title: 10 best herbal decoctions for dental plaque
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for dental plaque are a natural and effective way to combat plaque buildup on teeth. These decoctions are made by steeping herbs such as sage, chamomile, and peppermint in hot water, releasing their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce plaque formation and prevent tartar buildup. Regular consumption of these decoctions can improve oral health by reducing bad breath, gum inflammation, and tooth decay, ultimately improving overall confidence and quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with dental plaque because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The herb contains a compound called ursolic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause plaque formation. Additionally, purple coneflower has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums caused by plaque buildup. By drinking decoctions of this herb, individuals may be able to naturally maintain healthy teeth and gums, reducing the risk of dental problems like cavities and gum disease.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Purple coneflower's antimicrobial compounds, such as triterpenoids and phenolic acids, inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for dental plaque, preventing its formation and reducing existing buildup.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, including sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help reduce gum inflammation and swelling associated with dental plaque, promoting healthy gums and reducing oral pain.

Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower's rich antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals in the mouth, which can contribute to plaque formation and damage to teeth and gums, protecting them from oxidative stress and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce dental plaque by inhibiting the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a bacterium responsible for tooth decay and plaque formation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, in Echinacea purpurea decoctions have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which help combat the bacteria that contribute to dental plaque.

Caffeic acid: Caffeic acid, a phenolic acid found in Echinacea purpurea, has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce dental plaque and prevent gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections in the mouth.
Leaves: They have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi causing plaque.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that can help inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 to 60 grams of dried echinacea purpurea root and flowers to make the decoction.
Combine the gathered echinacea with 1 quart of boiling water to fill a medium-sized pot.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes or until the liquid reduces to half its original volume.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Store the prepared decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage decoctions helps with dental plaque because of its remarkable antibacterial properties, which effectively combat the buildup of bacteria that cause tartar and plaque on teeth. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in sage also reduce redness and swelling in gums, making it an excellent natural remedy for gum inflammation. Additionally, sage's astringent nature helps to dry out the mouth, reducing the amount of acid-producing bacteria that can contribute to tooth decay.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Sage's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol, inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that contribute to dental plaque formation, reducing the risk of oral infections.
Anti-inflammatory property: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, including rosmarinic acid, help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth tissues, which can lead to plaque buildup and gum disease.
Antioxidant property: Sage's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can damage teeth and gums, contributing to plaque formation and oral health issues.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that acts as an antimicrobial agent, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque.

Salvianolic acid A: A polyphenolic compound that exhibits antioxidant properties, which can help to neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress that can contribute to the formation of dental plaque.
Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid that has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria and inflammation associated with dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Salvia officinalis are commonly used due to their high concentration of antibacterial compounds that help reduce bacteria in the mouth.
Roots: The roots of the plant are also used for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help combat plaque and gum inflammation.
Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis contain compounds that help prevent bacterial growth, making them useful in decoctions for oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of fresh or 10 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves from mature plants.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep the leaves in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree decoctions helps with dental plaque because they contain antimicrobial properties that effectively combat bacteria responsible for its formation. The potent oils present in these decoctions, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a primary contributor to dental plaque. Regular consumption of tea tree decoction can help maintain healthy gums and teeth by reducing biofilm formation, promoting a clean mouth environment that is less susceptible to plaque accumulation.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Tea tree oil, a key component of herbal tea tree decoctions, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for dental plaque formation, reducing the risk of cavities and gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal tea tree decoctions contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, making them less susceptible to damage from plaque buildup.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in herbal tea tree decoctions can neutralize free radicals that can contribute to the formation of dental plaque and tartar, protecting teeth and gums from oxidative stress.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque.
Cymene: This terpene has antibacterial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can cause dental plaque and gum disease.
Linalool: This terpene has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antimicrobial properties, which help combat dental plaque and promote oral health.
Buds: Rich in bioactive compounds that exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective against dental plaque.
Barks: Contain compounds with antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and transfer them to a heat-resistant container.
Combine the dried leaves with 250ml of boiling water and stir gently to prevent foaming.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to facilitate the release of active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Transfer the filtered decoction to a glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with dental plaque because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation, while also reducing inflammation in the gums. Additionally, ginger has been found to improve saliva flow, which helps wash away food particles and debris that can accumulate on teeth, making it a natural and effective way to maintain good oral health and prevent dental plaque buildup.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums and mouth, thereby preventing the formation of dental plaque.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of ginger decoctions can help inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque, such as Streptococcus mutans, reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum disease.

Volatile oils: Ginger's volatile oils, like cineole and zingiberene, have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help eliminate plaque-causing bacteria and fungi, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These phenolic compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque, thereby preventing plaque formation and promoting oral health.

Shogaols: Shogaols, a class of sesquiterpenes, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a primary bacteria responsible for dental plaque and tooth decay, thereby reducing plaque formation and preventing dental caries.

Gingerol-6 and Gingerol-8 (6-Gingerol and 8-Gingerol): These two gingerols have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with dental plaque, thereby promoting a healthy oral environment and preventing plaque formation.
remedy_parts: Buds: Ginger buds are sometimes used in decoctions for their antibacterial properties, which help to eliminate bacteria that cause dental plaque and gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried zingiber officinale root per 8 ounces of water.
Combine the root with water in a saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to medium and simmer for 5 to 10 minutes or until the liquid is reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with dental plaque because of its potent antibacterial properties, which specifically target the bacteria that cause plaque formation. The curcuminoids present in turmeric have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a common culprit behind tooth decay and gingivitis. Additionally, turmeric's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce inflammation in the gums, making it easier for saliva to remove plaque and food particles from teeth, promoting overall oral health. Regular consumption of turmeric decoctions can contribute to a healthier, more radiant smile.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, which can contribute to plaque buildup and gum disease.
Antibacterial properties: Turmeric contains antimicrobial compounds like curcuminoids and turmerone, which inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause plaque formation and gum disease.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect oral tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to plaque accumulation and tooth decay.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation associated with dental plaque and gum disease.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a curcuminoid, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque, such as Streptococcus mutans.
Tumerone: Tumerone, a sesquiterpene, has been found to possess antibacterial properties that help combat the formation of dental plaque and prevent the development of gum disease.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high content of curcumin, a compound with antimicrobial properties that help reduce dental plaque.
Roots: They contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat dental plaque.
Barks: They have been traditionally used to make decoctions that help reduce dental plaque and gum inflammation, likely due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Wash 1-2 teaspoons of dried curcuma longa roots under cold running water for 30 seconds.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces using a sharp knife for 2 minutes.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan for 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup for 2 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with dental plaque because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat the bacteria that cause plaque buildup. The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce gum inflammation and swelling, making it an effective remedy for periodontal disease. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to inhibit the production of enzymes that contribute to plaque formation, helping to prevent the accumulation of tartar on teeth.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Licorice root has antimicrobial properties that can inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque, helping to reduce the formation of tartar and prevent gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in licorice root can help soothe and reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, making it less likely for plaque to build up and causing damage to teeth and gums.

Antioxidant property: Licorice root's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to oral tissues, reducing the risk of tooth decay and other oral health issues.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing the growth of dental plaque and preventing inflammation of the gums.

Phenolic acids (specifically, liquiritin and isofraxidin): These phenolic acids have been shown to inhibit the growth of oral bacteria, thereby reducing the formation of dental plaque and preventing conditions such as gingivitis.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpene saponin, has antimicrobial properties that help in reducing the population of oral bacteria responsible for dental plaque formation, thus preventing oral infections and promoting oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used in decoctions due to their content of flavonoids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects.
Barks: The barks are also used in decoctions due to their glycoside content, which helps in reducing plaque and inflammation in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 to 60 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots for decoction preparation.
Rinse the harvested roots with cold water to remove any impurities thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned roots into smaller pieces to increase surface area for decoction.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 to 3 liters of water in a large pot.
Boil the mixture for 10 to 20 minutes to extract the active compounds fully.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Neem decoctions helps with dental plaque because they possess potent antimicrobial properties that effectively eliminate bacteria responsible for plaque formation. The compounds present in neem, such as azadirachtin and nimbin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a primary contributor to dental plaque and tooth decay. Additionally, neem decoctions have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce gum inflammation and swelling associated with plaque buildup.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Neem's antimicrobial compounds, such as nimbin and nimbidic acid, inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque, reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Neem's anti-inflammatory compounds, including quercetin and kaempferol, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, making it less susceptible to damage caused by dental plaque and tartar buildup.

Antioxidant properties: Neem's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to the formation of dental plaque, promoting overall oral health and reducing oxidative stress.

Azadirachtin: This limonoid has been found to possess antibacterial properties, which help in reducing the bacterial load on the teeth, subsequently decreasing the accumulation of plaque.

Phenolic acids (such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid): These compounds have been demonstrated to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which help in mitigating the negative effects of oral bacteria and reducing the formation of plaque.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antimicrobial properties, which help reduce bacterial growth and prevent dental plaque.
Seeds: The seeds contain azadirachtin, a compound that exhibits antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in preventing dental plaque.
Barks: The barks of Azadirachta indica contain flavonoids and terpenoids that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to prevent and reduce dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried azadirachta indica leaves and 10-15 grams of dried root bark.
Crush 10 grams of dried azadirachta indica root bark into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the crushed root bark powder with 500ml of water in a saucepan and boil for 10-15 minutes.
Add the dried azadirachta indica leaves to the boiling mixture and continue to boil for another 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to discard solids.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of fresh petroselinum crispum leaves and 1 tablespoon of fresh flowers for decoction.
Boil 1 liter of water in a pot for 5 minutes to prepare the decoction base.
Combine the gathered petroselinum crispum leaves and flowers with the boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a bowl for 5 minutes.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days in an airtight container.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are commonly used for decoctions due to their high content of antibacterial compounds, such as thymol, which can help reduce dental plaque.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can help combat dental plaque and gum inflammation.
Roots: Thymus vulgaris roots are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce dental plaque and gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers from a clean and dry environment.
Cut the thymus vulgaris into smaller pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the cut thymus vulgaris with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes or until the liquid reaches a temperature of 95 degrees celsius.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedy_parts: Barks: Cinnamomum verum barks are commonly used to make decoctions due to their antimicrobial properties that help reduce plaque and prevent gum inflammation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest cinnamomum verum bark from mature trees using pruning shears to avoid damaging the tree.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm place for 2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine 2 teaspoons of the cinnamomum verum powder with 1 cup of boiling water for a decoction.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes and strain the liquid before consuming the herbal decoction.

remedy_recipe: Measure out 10-20 grams of dried red clover flowers trifolium pratense into a clean glass container.
Boil one liter of water in a pot and then reduce heat to a simmer for five minutes.
Add the measured red clover flowers to the hot water and let steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container.

Steep the mixture for exactly 10 minutes allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.

Barks: The barks of lavender contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial properties that can help combat dental plaque-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers according to your preferred method and store in an airtight container.
Combine one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes allowing the volatile oils to infuse into the water before straining the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids and let it cool to room temperature.
Store the cooled lavandula angustifolia decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours using as a mouthwash by swishing it around your mouth for one minute twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram decoctions helps with dental plaque because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The essential oils present in sweet marjoram, such as bornyl acetate and linalool, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation. By reducing the accumulation of bacteria on tooth surfaces, sweet marjoram decoctions help to prevent the development of dental plaque and tartar, ultimately promoting a healthy and clean smile.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Marjoram's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque formation, reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in marjoram, including flavonoids and terpenes, can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums and mouth, which is often associated with dental plaque buildup.

Antioxidant properties: Marjoram's antioxidant compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to the development of dental plaque and gum disease by reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of sweet marjoram have antibacterial properties that help combat bacteria responsible for dental plaque formation.
Barks: Sweet marjoram barks contain tannins, which are known for their ability to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried origanum majorana leaves measuring one teaspoon for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the herbs with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container to remove solids.
Allow the decoction to cool at room temperature before storing it in an airtight container for up to three days.
Rinse your mouth with one tablespoon of the cooled decoction three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with dental plaque because of its unique properties. The menthol in peppermint has antibacterial properties that can help reduce the formation of plaque by inhibiting the growth of bacteria. Additionally, the decoction's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe gums and reduce inflammation caused by plaque buildup. Furthermore, peppermint's refreshing flavor can stimulate saliva production, which helps to neutralize acids and remineralize teeth, making it an effective natural solution for maintaining good oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Peppermint's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that contribute to the formation of dental plaque, thereby preventing the onset of oral infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with gum disease, which can be caused by plaque buildup, allowing for a healthier gum environment.
Antioxidant activity: Peppermint's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to the development of dental plaque, thus promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the mouth, making it effective against dental plaque.
Barks: Peppermint barks contain menthol, which has antibacterial and antifungal properties that help combat dental plaque-causing bacteria and fungi.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves mentha x piperita for every 8 ounces water.
Bring the mixture to a boil in a medium saucepan over high heat within 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the decoction for 15-20 minutes at 200 degrees fahrenheit temperature.
Strain the peppermint leaves from the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth after 5 minutes.
Cool the herbal tea to room temperature and store it in an airtight container within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm decoctions helps with dental plaque because of its unique properties that allow it to effectively combat biofilm formation on teeth. The mucilage present in slippery elm has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation, while also reducing inflammation and promoting healthy gum tissue. Additionally, its soothing nature can help reduce gingivitis and promote a healthier mouth environment, making it an effective natural remedy for maintaining good oral hygiene.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm decoctions contain mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that can coat and soothe the gums, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial adhesion, thus reducing the formation of dental plaque.

Antimicrobial activity: The decoction has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties against various oral pathogens, including Streptococcus mutans, which is a common causative agent of dental caries and periodontal disease, thereby helping to prevent the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce gum swelling and redness associated with gingivitis and periodontitis, allowing for a healthier oral environment and reduced likelihood of dental plaque buildup.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of the slippery elm tree have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dental plaque.
Roots: Slippery elm roots contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe irritated gums and reduce bleeding caused by dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra bark, dried it thoroughly then grind into fine powder form.
Combine 20 grams of the powdered bark with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15 minutes, allowing the mixture to steep and release its properties.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding solids.
Allow the liquid to cool to room temperature before storing it in an airtight container for up to three days.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dental plaque that are treated with herbal decoctions are those affecting oral and throat health. Conditions such as gum inflammation, mouth ulcers, and tonsillitis can be alleviated by drinking herbal teas made from plants like tea tree, chamomile, and sage. These decoctions have antibacterial properties that help reduce swelling, soothe pain, and promote healing in the affected areas. Additionally, they may also help prevent further infection and promote overall oral health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with dental plaque is a blend of Neem, Turmeric, and Clove. Neem's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause plaque, while Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and prevent gum inflammation. Clove's analgesic properties ease pain and discomfort associated with plaque and gum disease. This combination can be made into a decoction by steeping equal parts of Neem, Turmeric, and Clove roots in hot water for 5-7 minutes.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The Journal of clinical dentistry, a single-blind investigation on 50 dentistry students aged 23-28 years found that herbal decoctions can be beneficial in reducing dental plaque. The study, which lasted for 4 weeks, observed a significant reduction in approximal plaque index (API) from 40.8% to 23.9% and sulcus bleeding index (SBI) from 33.4% to 18.6% in students who used preparations containing medicinal herbs, compared to those who used placebo preparations. This reduction in API and SBI suggests that herbal decoctions can be an effective tool in preventing and managing dental plaque.
